entries.name: Age
entries: Tieflings mature at the same rate as humans but live a few years longer.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Tieflings might not have an innate tendency toward evil, but many of them end up there. Evil or not, an independent nature inclines many tieflings toward a chaotic alignment.

entries.name: Size
entries: Tieflings are about the same size and build as humans. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Bestial Senses
entries: Your inherently beast-like senses granted to you by your beastblood allow you to gain either Darkvision or Keen Hearing and Smell.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

entries.name: Skeletal
entries: You are vulnerable to bludgeoning damage, but resistant to slashing and piercing damage.

entries.name: Magic Resistance
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

entries.name: Bestial Legacy
entries: You know the druidcraft cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the absorb elements spell as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the summon beast spell once with this trait, requiring no material component, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Infernal.

entries.name: Appearance
entries: Your tiefling might not look like other tieflings. Rather than having the physical characteristics described in the Player's Handbook, these are some of the example features that a Beastblood Tiefling may have: antler-like horns; boar tusks or sharp fangs; frog-like tongue; catlike eyes; fur spots on your body; goatlike legs; leathery or scaly skin; etc. Their skin color can be either the standard red, blue, purple, etc. skin colors that a tiefling may have or they can also have skin color closer to humans.

There exists in Thethus a type of Tiefling whose blood is not only of an infernal's but is also almost fey-like in nature but yet still different from an actual fey humanoid such as the Satyrs. Not much is known about the Beastblood Tieflings except for some of the conditions that can may or may not lead to their creation. These conditions are:
The Haverna Woodlands is the site of reproduction.
A humanoid procreating with another humanoid has a very low chance of creating a Beastblood Tiefling.
A humanoid procreating with a tiefling has a low chance of creating a Beastblood Tiefling.
A humanoid procreating with a beastkin has a decent of creating a Beastblood Tiefling.
A tiefling procreating with another tiefling has an improved chance of creating a Beastblood Tiefling.
A tiefling procreating with a beastkin has a great chance of creating a Beastblood Tiefling.

entries.name: Bestial Bloodline
entries: While most tieflings are of an infernal bloodline, beastblood tieflings are ones that are granted a heritage linked to the weird and mystical woodlands that is Haverna and it's beastkin people. This rare form of tieflings is easily recognizable, their horns usually bear close resemblance to ones that animals such as deers or rams have, almost having the same biological makeup to their animal counterparts. They sometimes have tails akin to lion's or other animals, which lash or coil around their legs when they get upset or nervous. They sometimes have tusks like a boar or the sharply pointed teeth that tigers have, and their eyes are solid colors black, red, white, silver, or gold with no visible sclera or pupil. Their skin tones cover the full range of human coloration and unlike the standard tiefling, the skin pigmentation of a beastblood tiefling cannot be red, blue, purple, or etc.

entries.name: Self-Reliant and Suspicious
entries: Tieflings subsist in small minorities found mostly in human cities or towns, often in the roughest quarters of those places, where they grow up to be swindlers, thieves or crime lords. Sometimes they live among other minority populations in enclaves where they are treated with more respect.

Lacking a homeland, tieflings know that they have to make their own way in the world and that they have to be strong to survive. They are not quick to trust anyone who claims to be a friend, but when a tiefling's companions demonstrate that they trust him or her, a tiefling learns to extend the same trust to them. And once a tiefling gives someone loyalty, the tiefling is a friend or ally for life.

entries.name: Tiefling Names
entries: Tiefling names fall into three broad categories. Tieflings born into another culture typically have names reflective of that culture. Some have names derived from the infernal language passed down through generations, and reflect their fiendish heritage. And some younger tieflings striving to find a place in the world, adopt a name that signifies a virtue or other concept and then try to embody that concept. For some, the chosen name is a quest. For others, its a grim destiny.

entries.name: Male Names
entries: Akmenos, Amnon, Harahas, Ekernon, Tados, Kairon, Leucis, Melech, Morthos, Pelaios, Skamos, Therai

entries.name: Female Names
entries: Akra, Anakis, Bryseis, Criella, Ea, Kallista, Lerissa, Makaria, Nemeia, Phelaia, Rieta

entries.name: Virture Names
entries: Art, Carrion, Chant, Creed, Despair, Fear, Glory, Hope, Ideal, Music, Nowhere, Poetry, Quest, Random, Reverence, Sorrow, Torment, Weary
